name: null_addr_valid
reason: Address zero (null pointer) should never be a valid 4K virtual address

name: empty_range_trivially_valid
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_range_trivially_valid(va: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_va_4k_range_valid(va, 0usize),
{
}
reason: A zero-length range being unconditionally valid could mask bugs where callers assume non-empty ranges

name: add_range_not_injective
code:
proof fn phi_3_add_range_not_injective(va: usize, i: usize, j: usize)
    requires
        spec_va_4k_valid(va),
        va + 100 * 4096 < usize::MAX,
        0 <= i < 100,
        0 <= j < 100,
        i != j,
    ensures
        spec_va_add_range(va, i) == spec_va_add_range(va, j),
{
}
reason: Distinct offsets mapping to the same address would break no_duplicates invariant of VaRange4K

name: userspace_addr_accepted
reason: A low user-space address (L4 index 0) passing kernel-space validation would be a security violation

name: new_zero_len_wf
reason: VaRange4K::new accepting len=0 and producing a trivially well-formed empty range could hide logic errors in callers that assume non-empty ranges
